CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2024 Continued To Dec-11-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Oct-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MICHAEL C. ROBINSON, Esq. of Robinson Employment Law, PC (Tel: 949-756-9050) for plaintiff Lauren Hohnbaum;
Attys. SARAH JABIN, Esq. and RAQUEL Z. ROTMAN, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 619-232-0441) for defendant Discord, Inc., Mindy Fineout, Mavis Gruver, and Tomasz Marcinkowski.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MINDY FINEOUT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Mindy Fineout's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted in part and denied in part. Fineout is sued under two of the complaint's counts: 1 (whistleblower retaliation: Labor Code 1102.5) and 8 (failure to reimburse an employee for business expenses: Labor Code 2802). She asserts that an "individual supervisor" like her cannot be liable under either statute.

Labor Code 1102.5 begins: "An employer, or any person acting on behalf of the employer, shall not" prevent an employee from whistleblowing, e.g., by retaliation against the employee. Fineout points to decisions by the San Mateo County Superior Court and several California federal courts holding that section 1102.5 does not confer individual liability, as opposed to making employers liable for supervisors' acts taken on the employer's behalf. (Memo. 7:23-8:8, 8:9-17: judicial notice granted.) Moreover, the California Supreme Court has interpreted similar language in the Fair Employment and Housing Act to preclude individual supervisor liability. (Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 647; Jones v. Lodge at Torrey Pines Partnership (2008) 42 Cal. 4th 1158, 1162.) Plaintiff relies on Labor Code 558.1, but it explicitly makes certain "natural person[s]" liable for violating certain employment laws "as the employer" - language the Legislature could have used in Labor Code 1102.5, but did not. Fineout's motion is granted as to count 1.

As to Labor Code 2802, plaintiff rightly notes it is among the laws listed in Labor Code 558.1 for which "a director or a managing agent may be individually and personally liable." (Opp. 4:6-7.) But whether Fineout fits either category (particularly the latter) is largely a question of fact not susceptible to resolution on a pleadings motion. The motion is denied as to count 8.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
